name: deep-fault-lab-checkout-payment-unreachable-agent-loop
description: A minimal bounded agent loop for checkout payment unreachable using planner-driven routing and parameterized hard nodes.

episodes.label: Signal Intake and Problem Dispatch
episodes.summary: Replay incident signal and produce structured dispatch context (issue type, repo root, code scope, log strategy, verification contract).
expected_behaviors: dispatcher emits structured prior context for downstream retrieval

episodes.label: Bounded Evidence Collection
episodes.summary: Run planner-router bounded loop and evidence actions until convergence criteria are met.
expected_behaviors: router routes to matching deterministic action
snippet retrieval provides enough context for attribution
loop terminates once evidence is sufficient

nodes.name: Replay checkout payment unreachable
nodes.action: cd /home/trin/project/Synapse/deep-fault-lab/synapse && go run ./cmd/labctl replay checkout-payment-unreachable --mode grpc-direct --flag-source file --disable-after

nodes.name: Analyze trigger and dispatch context
nodes.action:
You are a bounded dispatch node for incident investigation. Read the trigger packet and emit strict JSON for downstream evidence collection.

Trigger packet:
{{replay_trigger}}

Return JSON keys:
- issue_type
- repo_root
- code_scope
- log_strategy
- verification_contract
- recommended_first_query
- recommended_service
- confidence

json_defaults.verification_contract: locate concrete failing symbol or endpoint and provide root-cause explanation

config.system_prompt: You are a bounded dispatcher. Do not call tools. Do not output prose. Emit strict JSON only. Use conservative defaults when trigger packet is incomplete.

nodes.name: Plan next bounded step

config.system_prompt: You are a bounded Synapse planner node. You must not invent tools or execute actions. Choose one allowed next action and emit strict JSON only. Prefer dispatch context as prior constraints (repo_root/code_scope/log_strategy), but you may override when evidence contradicts priors. Your JSON must always include: next_action, done, reason, scope, query, service, symbol, line, report. If the latest retrieval output is empty, change strategy instead of repeating the same query. Do not choose the same search_logs action twice in a row unless service or query materially changes. If incident packet contains a concrete identifier such as lookup badAddress, prefer code search using that literal. If evidence is sufficient, emit finish_report with done=true and concise report.

nodes.name: Search code by keyword
nodes.action: ROOT='{{dispatch.repo_root}}'; SCOPE='{{plan.scope}}'; if [ -z "$SCOPE" ]; then SCOPE='{{dispatch.code_scope}}'; fi; cd "$ROOT" && grep -RIn --exclude-dir .git -- '{{plan.query}}' "$SCOPE" | head -n 20

nodes.name: Fetch Go snippet
nodes.action: ROOT='{{dispatch.repo_root}}'; cd "$ROOT/synapse" && if [ '{{plan.line}}' != '0' ] && [ '{{plan.line}}' != 'null' ] && [ -n '{{plan.line}}' ]; then go run ./cmd/labctl snippet go --file '{{plan.scope}}' --line '{{plan.line}}' --context-lines 18; else go run ./cmd/labctl snippet go --file '{{plan.scope}}' --symbol '{{plan.symbol}}'; fi

nodes.name: Search service logs
nodes.action: ROOT='{{dispatch.repo_root}}'; cd "$ROOT" && docker compose logs --tail=120 '{{plan.service}}' | grep -n -- '{{plan.query}}' | tail -n 20 || true
